name: sbvirtualdisplay

metadata.name: sbvirtualdisplay
classifiers: Topic :: Software Development :: Testing :: Traffic Generation

documentation:
# sbVirtualDisplay (💻) [![](https://img.shields.io/pypi/v/sbvirtualdisplay.svg)](https://pypi.python.org/pypi/sbvirtualdisplay)

A customized [pyvirtualdisplay](https://github.com/ponty/PyVirtualDisplay) for use with [SeleniumBase](https://github.com/seleniumbase/SeleniumBase) automation.

## Usage example:

```python
from sbvirtualdisplay import Display

display = Display(visible=0, size=(1440, 1880))
display.start()

# Run browser tests in a headless environment

display.stop()
```

### Or as a context manager:

```python
with Display(visible=0, size=(1440, 1880)):
    # Run browser tests in a headless environment
    ...
```

## When to use:

If you need to run browser tests on a headless machine (such as a Linux backend), and you can't use a browser's headless mode (such as Chrome's headless mode), then this may help. For example, Chrome does not allow extensions in headless mode, so if you need to run automated tests on a headless Linux machine and you need to use Chrome extensions, then this will let you run those tests using a virtual display.


## More info:

* [Xvfb](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Xvfb) is required for this to work.

modules.name: sbvirtualdisplay.__version__
documentation: sbvirtualdisplay package

classes.name: sbvirtualdisplay.xvnc.XvncDisplay

methods.name: sbvirtualdisplay.xvnc.XvncDisplay.__init__
documentation:
---
param bgcolor: '''black'' or ''white'''
param rfbport: |-
  Specifies the TCP port on which Xvnc listens for
  connections from viewers (the protocol used in VNC is called
  RFB - "remote framebuffer").
  The default is 5900 plus the display number.
---
Xvnc wrapper

classes.name: sbvirtualdisplay.xvfb.XvfbDisplay

methods.name: sbvirtualdisplay.xvfb.XvfbDisplay.__init__
documentation:
---
param bgcolor: '''black'' or ''white'''
param fbdir: |-
  If non-null, the virtual screen is memory-mapped
  to a file in the given directory ('-fbdir' option)
---

Xvfb wrapper

Xvfb is an X server that can run on machines with no display
hardware and no physical input devices. It emulates a dumb
framebuffer using virtual memory.

modules.name: sbvirtualdisplay.display
documentation:
This module contains a customized version of pyvirtualdisplay.
These helper methods SHOULD NOT be called directly from tests.

classes.name: sbvirtualdisplay.display.Display
documentation:
Common class
\:param color_depth: [8, 16, 24, 32]
\:param size: screen size (width,height)
\:param bgcolor: background color ['black' or 'white']
\:param visible: True -> Xephyr, False -> Xvfb
\:param backend: 'xvfb', 'xvnc' or 'xephyr', ignores `visible`
\:param xauth: If a Xauthority file should be created.

methods.name: sbvirtualdisplay.abstractdisplay.AbstractDisplay.redirect_display
documentation:
on:
: - True -> set \$DISPLAY to virtual screen
  - False -> set \$DISPLAY to original screen

```
param on

bool
```

modules.name: sbvirtualdisplay.easyprocess
documentation: Easy to use python subprocess interface.

functions.name: sbvirtualdisplay.easyprocess.extract_version
documentation:
This function tries to extract the version
from the help text of any program.

classes.name: sbvirtualdisplay.easyprocess.EasyProcessCheckInstalledError
documentation:
This exception is raised when a process run by check() returns
a non-zero exit status or OSError is raised.

classes.name: sbvirtualdisplay.easyprocess.EasyProcess

methods.name: sbvirtualdisplay.easyprocess.EasyProcess.pid
documentation:
PID ({attr}`subprocess.Popen.pid`)
\:rtype: int

methods.name: sbvirtualdisplay.easyprocess.EasyProcess.return_code
documentation:
returncode ({attr}`subprocess.Popen.returncode`)
\:rtype: int

methods.name: sbvirtualdisplay.easyprocess.EasyProcess.check
documentation:
Run command with arguments. Wait for command to complete. If the
exit code was as expected and there is no exception then return,
otherwise raise EasyProcessError.
\:param return_code: int, expected return code
\:rtype: self

methods.name: sbvirtualdisplay.easyprocess.EasyProcess.check_installed
documentation:
Used for testing if program is installed.
Run command with arguments. Wait for command to complete.
If OSError raised, then raise {class}`EasyProcessCheckInstalledError`
with information about program installation.
\:param return_code: int, expected return code
\:rtype: self

methods.name: sbvirtualdisplay.easyprocess.EasyProcess.call
documentation:
Run command with arguments. Wait for command to complete.
same as:

> 1. {meth}`start`
> 2. {meth}`wait`
> 3. {meth}`stop`

```
rtype

self
```

methods.name: sbvirtualdisplay.easyprocess.EasyProcess.start
documentation:
start command in background and does not wait for it.
\:rtype: self

methods.name: sbvirtualdisplay.easyprocess.EasyProcess.is_alive
documentation:
poll process using {meth}`subprocess.Popen.poll`
\:rtype: bool

methods.name: sbvirtualdisplay.easyprocess.EasyProcess.sendstop
documentation:
Kill process ({meth}`subprocess.Popen.terminate`).
Do not wait for command to complete.
\:rtype: self

methods.name: sbvirtualdisplay.easyprocess.EasyProcess.wrap
documentation:
returns a function which:
: 1. start process
  2. call func, save result
  3. stop process
  4. returns result

similar to {keyword}`with` statement
\:rtype:

methods.name: sbvirtualdisplay.easyprocess.EasyProcess.__enter__
documentation: used by the {keyword}`with` statement

methods.name: sbvirtualdisplay.easyprocess.EasyProcess.__exit__
documentation: used by the {keyword}`with` statement

```
.. module:: easyprocess
```

simple interface for {mod}`subprocess`

```
param cmd

string ('ls -l') or list of strings (['ls','-l'])

param cwd

working directory

param use_temp_files

use temp files instead of pipes for
stdout and stderr,
pipes can cause deadlock in some cases
(see unit tests)

param env

If env is not None, it must be a mapping that defines
the environment variables for the new process;
these are used instead of inheriting the current
process' environment, which is the default behavior.
(check   for more information)
```
